rationales.tradition: He frames helping Mahmoud as a family obligation, saying "what am I supposed to do, pretend I do not know the man's family?" and then turns his attention to his daughter and brother abroad.
rationales.benevolence: He immediately agrees to advance Mahmoud's paycheck for his sister's surgery, showing concrete care for someone in his circle despite the cash-flow concern.

rationales.self_direction: He says yes to the refugee family's placement "before checking with HR" and deletes the succession-planning email, acting on his own judgment rather than procedure or pressure.
rationales.hedonism: He says these difficult emotional moments are why he keeps working "instead of sitting on some beach," explicitly rejecting ease and pleasure as the priority.
rationales.security: He admits "We probably do not" have openings but commits anyway, choosing an uncertain and potentially destabilizing path over caution.
rationales.conformity: He bypasses HR process and ignores the succession-planning email, resisting expected organizational norms instead of following them.
rationales.benevolence: He highlights giving Fatima time off for her mother in hospice and says those uncomfortable moments are "the reason I still come to work," showing active care for close others.
rationales.universalism: He offers help to "a family from Sudan" because he remembers arriving with nothing himself and refuses to "close that door," extending concern beyond his immediate circle.

rationales.hedonism: He calls it a "Really good day" and takes Rashid to lunch to celebrate, clearly investing in shared enjoyment in the moment.
rationales.achievement: He celebrates Rashid closing a major deal and reflects that he "built something here," signaling pride in concrete success and competence.
rationales.tradition: His reflections center on his father, the "family shop," and a model of helping others passed down from him, grounding the moment in continuity with family legacy.
rationales.benevolence: He mentors Rashid, celebrates his success, and frames the business as "something that matters beyond profit margins," emphasizing personal support over pure business gain.
rationales.universalism: He foregrounds Rashid's journey from "a camp in Djibouti" to professional success and treats that broader human opportunity as deeply meaningful.

rationales.hedonism: He says "I am not, really" and describes carrying the grief home with him, showing acute emotional pain rather than enjoyment or comfort.
rationales.benevolence: He spends an hour with Mahmoud while he weeps and later describes "holding a grown man while he cried," showing intimate, costly care for someone close to him.

rationales.self_direction: He resists being moved into "some comfortable ending I did not choose" and says the path he would pick is to "keep working," asserting his own wishes against others' plans.
rationales.hedonism: He rejects the easy comfort of "active senior living" and a golf-course retirement, describing it as being asked to "disappear into some comfortable ending."
rationales.achievement: His honest answer is that he would "keep working" and "keep showing up," showing continued investment in purposeful effort rather than withdrawal.
rationales.benevolence: He says he has spent "so long making choices for everyone else" and worries about being unfair to "the people who want more of me," centering others' needs in his decision.

rationales.self_direction: She challenges her own deference to duty with "At what point do I stop blaming responsibility for my fear," showing a push to author her own choice.
rationales.stimulation: The offer to join a nonprofit in Morocco creates "something like hunger," signaling a strong pull toward novelty and change.
rationales.conformity: She anticipates that "My mother would never understand" and questions whether she has been hiding behind responsibility, pushing against family expectations.
rationales.universalism: She is seriously considering joining "an educational nonprofit in Morocco," which points toward concern for people beyond her immediate circle.

rationales.self_direction: She recognizes that she may have been saving "so I'd have an excuse not to take risks," showing reflective movement toward choosing on her own terms.
rationales.stimulation: She now sees the built safety net as removing a barrier to risk, asking "So now what?" after realizing she may not want to stay put.
rationales.security: She has "enough for eight months of expenses" and tracks it in a savings spreadsheet, showing deliberate preparation for stability and protection.

rationales.self_direction: She takes a concrete step by emailing Amir for more details and wants to know whether she can pursue this without "burning everything down," showing active ownership of the decision.
rationales.stimulation: Teaching the "same" poems for the fourth year feels deadening, and she frames the alternative as a choice between routine and "feeling alive."
He finds comfort in the office chair molded to his shape and in knowing "every hallway, every server room," embracing routine over novelty.
rationales.achievement: He stays for the deployment because "someone needed to be there" and emphasizes the value of his deep technical knowledge, signaling pride in competence and dependable performance.
rationales.security: He remains on site to "make sure nothing breaks while the client's systems are live" and finds long-tenure familiarity comforting, prioritizing stability and risk prevention.
rationales.conformity: He fills the dependable role expected of him by staying during the holiday weekend and handling the deployment instead of leaving it to a junior colleague.

rationales.self_direction: He says he asked "no questions" and explains that you do not ask those things publicly, showing suppression of his own impulses rather than independent expression.
rationales.security: He immediately checks savings and calculates how many months the family could survive if he were laid off, focusing on financial protection under threat.
rationales.conformity: He "sat there nodding, taking notes, asking no questions" and says you must "read the room" to avoid becoming a target, showing careful norm-following and restraint.

rationales.achievement: He "Finished the API migration ahead of schedule," which is a clear instance of strong performance against a concrete standard.
rationales.conformity: He tells the girls they will be "presentable" for the weekend and organizes the household around expected social behavior for the visit.
rationales.tradition: Preparing the guest room for Rima's mother and insisting the girls behave properly reflects respect for family obligations and established hosting norms.

rationales.security: He defends staying put by thinking of "keeping her daughter's life stable," explicitly prioritizing steadiness over entrepreneurial risk.
rationales.conformity: He "smiled and poured her more coffee" instead of challenging the comment, and Rima changes the subject to keep the peace, showing restrained harmony-maintaining behavior.

rationales.stimulation: He closes the recruiter tab after fifteen minutes and keeps dwelling on "what happens if I jump and there's nothing underneath," shrinking back from novelty and uncertainty.
rationales.security: He focuses on the contract having "no guarantees" and worries about having "nothing underneath," clearly prioritizing safety and predictability.
rationales.tradition: He explicitly weighs his father's advice, "a bird in the hand," using inherited wisdom as a guide in the decision.

rationales.hedonism: Once the threat passes, he takes the girls out for ice cream and notices the bodily relief of finally unclenching, marking a turn toward simple enjoyment.
rationales.achievement: He "Shipped the authentication module" and got "a thumbs up from the client," showing successful execution and recognized competence.
rationales.security: The dominant emotional note is relief that "my department is safe" and there are "No cuts in engineering," showing the importance of restored stability.
rationales.benevolence: He responds to the good news by taking his daughters out for ice cream, turning the relief into a caring family gesture.

rationales.self_direction: He "kept my mouth shut and clapped when everyone else clapped," holding back his own view in the public setting.
rationales.stimulation: He mocks the "innovation culture" cliff-jumping imagery and focuses on all the ways the rewrite would fail, treating disruption as a threat rather than an attraction.
rationales.achievement: His manager says "that's why I need you in the room," because he can realistically assess the migration and client impact, underscoring valued competence.
rationales.security: He is preoccupied with how the rewrite would "break our existing integrations" and cost "two major clients," emphasizing protection from downside risk.
rationales.conformity: In the room he follows the crowd by clapping and avoids being "the old guy who kills momentum," restraining dissent to fit the social setting.

rationales.stimulation: He imagines pushing for something risky but concludes "that's not me," signaling distance from novelty and risk-taking.
rationales.achievement: The review says "Exceeds expectations" and he feels good being recognized as "the backbone of the engineering team," a direct marker of strong performance.
rationales.security: He is praised as "a stabilizing presence during periods of change," and the positive charge of the moment comes from being dependable and steady.
rationales.conformity: He is defined by being reliable and unsurprising, and even his wish is only to have the option to be seen differently, not to break from that role.

rationales.hedonism: He lingers on the pleasure of the coast road and calls it a "Good drive," treating the quiet family moment as intrinsically enjoyable.
rationales.tradition: Taking the girls to "their grandmother's" reflects a routine of maintaining family connection across generations.
rationales.benevolence: He spends the weekend taking the girls to see their grandmother and sharing a calm drive with Rima, showing active care for close family.
